questions.text: As someone who's chosen the digital nomad life in Mexico, what's your approach to visa logistics and immigration? Do you do border runs, have a temporary resident visa, fly under tourist status, or is it something you just figure out as you go?

When you're in a place and things are going well — you have good friends, a solid routine, a nice spot — what actually makes you decide to leave and move to a new city? Is there a specific feeling, timeframe, or trigger that signals it's time to move on?

How do you think about networking and professional connections while being location-independent? Do you make an effort to meet other remote workers or developers in the places you stay, or is that not a priority for you?

When it comes to managing money across countries — receiving USD payments, converting to Mexican pesos, handling banking as a US citizen abroad — how do you actually do it? Any apps, strategies, or banks you recommend?

Beyond volleyball and your daily movement, how do you think about recovery and rest? Are you someone who actively prioritizes rest days, stretching, mobility work, or is that not part of your routine?
